item.title: What I'd look like in a Hallmark Christmas movie
item.description: Time for my annual tradition of writing, directing, and starring in my own Hallmark movie :) This is my 4th year making these videos and I love doing them more and more every time lol Have a beautiful Sunday, friends! Happy December 1st!
item.dc:creator: GingerSnark

item.title: Every AM & PM I send my 2 adult sons and their partners memes. Jams, dad jokes, bizarre stuff, and some sillies. Here is the second half of the latest batch from this week…the movie ones.
item.description:
Every night I end my post with my kids with “I love you. I’m proud of you. I’m grateful you’re in my life.” And then I add a fourth, random thing. This night’s was: Learn to dance.
Beware cats from space.
Ah, dad joke commercials.
First: this mom is mega hottie. Second: she sure can lip sync. Third: she has damn good taste in music.
Beware the cats. Catnado. This is what I’ve been telling you.
Ah’ve gawt skooel!
80’s movies are fun. Like a vampire movie that just decides to have a beach party with an oiled up muscle man playing a sax and singing a one hit wonder / banger that has zero to do with the plot.
Beware the cats. They ride the roombas around the house when we aren’t home.
Beware the cats. Some of them are assholes.
Beware the cats.
Dad jokes! Hoo ha ha.
I bet these guys have their ducks in a row, too.
Beware the cats.
Aye aye aye.
Words of affirmation
item.dc:creator: Wheretheheckisjason

item.title: Judge me if you want
item.description: I’ve been a gamer my whole life. Somewhere along the line, I figured out I prefer to be immersed in the story of a game instead of the harder difficulty settings. I’ve had several friends tell me I’m “ruining” my experience by making it too easy, but I have way more fun playing the story than getting stuck on one super hard part. Judge me if you like… but I feel like playing for the story is just as legit as playing for difficulty #gaming #difficulty
item.dc:creator: JonnyRockstar11

item.title: I'm so proud of my leftover turkey pot pie! My recipe is below!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/leftover-turkey-pot-pie/ I still have turkey leftover from Thanksgiving, so I made a rich and delicious turkey pot pie! I'm so proud of it!! Full recipe below, but if you want more pictures and a deeper explanation, you can click the link above for all the details!! If you don't want to fuss with making the crust, just use two 9-inch store-bought pie crusts! Pie crust: 1½ cups all-purpose flour ½ cup cake flour ½ teaspoon kosher salt ¾ cups unsalted butter, 1½ sticks, chilled and small-diced ¼ cup shortening chilled ½ cup cold water Turkey pot pie filling: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil olive oil 2 cups diced vegetables such as carrots, celery, and onion 8 ounces russet potatoes approximately 2 medium-sized potatoes, peeled and small-diced 1½ teaspoons kosher salt plus more to taste 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1 teaspoon dry thyme 3 tablespoons butter ⅓ cup all-purpose flour 1½ cups chicken broth 1 cup whole milk 1 cup Gruyère cheese freshly grated ¼ teaspoon nutmeg ¼ teaspoon white pepper 2 cups cooked turkey diced 1 cup frozen peas Salt and pepper to taste Egg wash: 1 egg beaten 1 tablespoon whole milk Make the pie crust: Combine the all-purpose flour, cake flour, and kosher salt in a large bowl and use a fork to mix them. Add the cold, diced butter and the chilled shortening to the bowl of flour. Use a pastry cutter to blend the fat into the flour until a crumbly, coarse mixture forms. Mix the cold water into the flour until a dough ball forms. Don’t worry if there are a few loose crumbles. Use your hands to pull everything together into a dough ball. Dump the dough onto a large piece of plastic wrap and use your hands to gather any loose crumbs into the dough ball. Wrap the plastic wrap tightly around the ball of dough. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days. Start the pot pie filling: While the dough chills, make the pot pie filling. Heat 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil in a wide pot over medium heat. Add the diced carrots, celery, and onion. Sauté for 5 to 7 minutes until they soften. Add the diced potatoes and season with 1 teaspoon kosher salt, a big pinch of black pepper, garlic powder, and dry thyme. Sauté over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until the potatoes soften. Toast the flour: Melt the butter into the vegetables. Once bubbly, add the flour and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes until the flour becomes nutty and aromatic. Add the liquid: Add half the chicken broth to the pot and whisk to incorporate. Continue whisking until a slurry forms. Add the remaining chicken broth and continue whisking until smooth. Add the milk and whisk until smooth. Cook over medium heat until bubbly and thick. Add the cheese: Add the freshly grated Gruyère cheese and whisk until melted and smooth. Add the nutmeg and white pepper. Taste and add more salt if needed. Finish the pot pie filling: Add the cooked turkey and frozen peas and stir to incorporate. Simmer for 5 minutes until the peas are bright green. Turn off the heat. Cool the pot pie filling: Transfer the pot pie filling to a wide dish. Cover loosely with cling wrap and let stand for at least 30 minutes to 1 hour. I recommend transferring it to a wider dish instead of a deep bowl for a more rapid cool-down. See Note 1. Roll out the pie crust: Preheat the oven to 425ºF. Mix the beaten egg and the milk in a bowl and set aside. Remove the chilled dough from the refrigerator. Cut the ball in half and return half of the dough to the refrigerator. Place the other half on a lightly floured surface and press it into a disc. Use a floured rolling pin to roll the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than your pie dish. Carefully lift the dough from the counter and place it into a 9-inch pie dish. See Note 2. Add another sprinkle of flour to your work surface. Roll out the other half of the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than the pie dish. Make the pot pie: Pour the cooled pot pie filling into the prepared pie dish. Carefully lift the top crust from your surface and place it on top. Trim off any excess and discard it or use it for decoration. Use your fingers to seal the pie crust edges. Crimp the crust with your fingers or a fork if you like. Use a sharp knife to create a few small slits in the top of the crust to allow the steam to escape. Brush the crust with the egg and milk mixture. You likely won’t use all of it. Bake the pot pie: Place the pie dish on a large baking sheet. Transfer to the oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and flaky. Rotate the pie once midway through baking for even browning. Remove from the oven and let stand for 20 minutes. To serve: Cut the pie into 8 slices and transfer to plates. Serve and enjoy! Notes Note 1: The pot pie filling should be no warmer than 50ºF. After it stands for 30 minutes to 1 hour, you can either proceed with making the pot pie or refrigerate the filling for up to 1 day before making the pot pie. For food safety reasons, do not let the pie filling stand at room temperature for more than 2 hours. Note 2: Though unnecessary, you may wish to lightly grease the pie dish with a touch of shortening. I add a very small amount of shortening for extra security against the crust sticking to the dish.
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes

item.title: Hexia profile
item.description: N JANUARY IM HOPING TO RELEASE A PROJECT PITCH. EITHER A COMIC OR AN ANIMATED PILOT PITCH! You’ll have to let me know what you’d like!!! And I’ll have to consider what’s most doable for me…BUT I REALLY WANNA DO IT GUYS. ITS MY DREAM TO HAVE A FULFILLED PROJECT BUT OBVIOUSLY ART IS HARD! It’s still my hearttaker idea, but redone and expanded. Thinking of doing a kickstarter with rewards or something, so at minimum I would have a concept art pitch book ?? Maybe? Feel free to hit me with ideas! I have a December discount on commissions atm!! https://ko-fi.com/miagabriella/link/CHRISTMAS

item.title: Here are six strategies that can help Democrats and Trump critics not only survive the coming four years, but come out stronger.
item.description: 1. Don’t Panic — Autocracy Takes Time America will not turn into a dictatorship overnight — or in four years. Even the most determined strongmen face internal hurdles, from the bureaucracy to the media and the courts. It took Erdoğan well over a decade to fully consolidate his power. Hungary’s Viktor Orbán and Poland’s Law and Justice Party needed years to erode democratic norms and fortify their grip on state institutions. 2. Don’t Disengage — Stay Connected The answer to political defeat is not to disconnect, but to organize. You can take a couple of days or weeks off, commiserate with friends and mute Elon Musk on X — or erase the app altogether. But in the end, the best way to develop emotional resilience is greater engagement. 3. Don’t Fear the Infighting In Turkey, Hungary and Poland, it was only after the opposition parties faced their strategic and ideological misalignment with society that they were able to begin to effectively fight back. 4. Charismatic Leadership Is a Non-Negotiable You will lose if you don’t find a captivating leader, as was the case in 2023 general elections in Turkey and in 2022 in Hungary. 5. Skip the Protests and Identity Politics For the longest time in Turkey, the opposition made the mistake of relying too much on holding street demonstrations and promoting secularism, Turkey’s version of identity politics, which speaks to the urban professional and middle class but not beyond. When Erdoğan finally lost his absolute predominance in Turkish politics in 2024, it was largely because of his mismanagement of the economy and the opposition’s growing competence in that area. 6. Have Hope In a country with a strong, decentralized system of government and with a long-standing tradition of free speech, the rule of law should be far more resilient than anywhere in the world. https://www.politico.com/news/magazine/2024/12/01/anti-trumpists-guide-next-four-years-00191724

item.title: another crazy dumpy dump of gloriouslessly stolen things mostly from reddit

Blue car please - Red one is nice too
Coin sorter from the 1960s
One way visibility tent - I'll merely use this for photos.
Satisfying winter scenes. It's that time of the year coming back again.
5 : So Satisfying bread cutting
This rotatable mood control ring
Creating a reflection of a reflection with water
Knife cutting through dumpling dough like it’s cotton candy
9 : UK Crap VS Peak Italian Engineering - I can totally relate !
Who's the master now ? --- Cat Tax. Yep those pussies look a bit strange.

item.dc:creator: SechalKhachan

item.title: He called me Dad….
item.description: So over a year ago now we took in a little boy. He was family but he was neglected and CPS was stepping in. We have full physical and legal custody of him until the age of 18. Tonight he was going to bed and I said “Goodnight, buddy. I love you.” Like I usually do. Tonight he said “Goodnight, Dad…Love you, Dad.” He could have asked for a Lambo at that moment and I would asked what color.

item.title: Okay, I've been thinking.
item.description:
When life gives you lemons, don't make lemonade! Get MAD!
I don’t want your damn lemons! What am I supposed to do with these?
Demand to see life’s manager! Make life rue the day it thought it could give Cave Johnson lemons! DO YOU KNOW WHO I AM?!?!
I’m the man who’s gonna BURN YOUR HOUSE DOWN! With the LEMONS!
I’m gonna get my engineers to invent a combustible lemon that BURNS YOUR HOUSE DOWN!!!
